overview: BPF activity on July 10-11 focused on JIT memory management and trampoline correctness improvements. Mike Rapoport submitted v2 of a five-patch series enabling EXECMEM_ROX_CACHE for BPF JIT allocations on x86, tightening memory permissions by moving JIT code to read-only executable pages. Yonghong Song's series fixed 128-bit value handling in trampolines, while Xu Kuohai removed redundant clone/restore overhead introduced during constants blinding.

patches.title: x86/bpf: enable EXECMEM_ROX_CACHE for BPF allocations
patches.author: Mike Rapoport
patches.summary: This patch enables the EXECMEM_ROX_CACHE allocator for BPF JIT allocations on x86_64, replacing the previous writable-then-executable allocation approach with read-only executable (ROX) memory. By leveraging the execmem ROX cache, BPF JIT-compiled programs run from pages that are never simultaneously writable, strengthening kernel memory security. The series (v2) consists of preparatory steps including vmalloc-based rw_image allocation, removal of weak JIT alloc/free symbols, and ROX-awareness in alloc_prog_pack before this final enablement.

patches.title: bpf: Reject >8 byte return values on return-reading trampoline paths
patches.author: Yonghong Song
patches.summary: The BPF trampoline infrastructure does not properly handle functions returning values wider than 8 bytes such as 128-bit integers, leading to potential stack corruption on fexit/fmod_ret paths. This patch adds a verifier-level check to reject programs that attempt to read return values wider than 8 bytes via trampolines. The companion x86 trampoline fix ensures the stack layout correctly accounts for 128-bit arguments passed in register pairs, and selftests cover the new behavior across both cases.

patches.title: bpf: Eliminate dup/restore of insn_aux_data
patches.author: Xu Kuohai
patches.summary: During constants blinding, the BPF verifier duplicates insn_aux_data and the program image before patching, then restores them if blinding turns out to be unnecessary. This patch eliminates the unnecessary duplication of insn_aux_data by restructuring the blinding path so that the auxiliary data is only modified when blinding is actually applied. The companion patch removes the corresponding dup/restore of subprog_starts and the prog clone, reducing transient memory allocation overhead in the constants blinding path.

patches.title: bpf: Enable JIT hardening by default when x86_64 CFI is enabled
patches.author: Jennifer Miller
patches.summary: This RFC proposes automatically enabling BPF JIT hardening (bpf_jit_harden=1) on x86_64 systems that boot with Control Flow Integrity (CFI) active, since both features share the goal of hardening indirect control flow and CFI-enabled kernels already carry the associated overhead. JIT hardening provides additional protection against BPF constant blinding attacks, making it a natural complement to CFI. The patch is an RFC seeking community feedback on the policy before wider adoption.

patches.title: bpf: Mark tracing_multi trampolines as ftrace managed
patches.author: Leon Hwang
patches.summary: When multiple BPF programs are attached via tracing_multi links, the associated trampolines must be properly marked as ftrace-managed to ensure correct detach sequencing. Without this marking, detaching a fentry link after a tracing_multi link on the same target function triggers a WARNING in bpf_trampoline_multi_detach due to inconsistent reference state. This patch fixes the marking flag and the companion selftest reproduces the problematic attach/detach sequence.

patches.title: bpf: Fix unused nskb warning in bpf_icmp_send
patches.author: Mahe Tardy
patches.summary: A compiler warning about an unused nskb variable was introduced in the bpf_icmp_send helper under certain kernel configurations. This single-patch fix adjusts the variable declaration so it is properly consumed in all compilation paths, eliminating the warning without any change in runtime behavior. The fix is a minor cleanup targeting code quality in the BPF network helper layer.
